exists(string).details: This function will return a boolean indicating if the contract was found.

params.name: The name of the contract to check.

returns._0: exists A boolean indicating if the contract was found.

getContract(string).details: This function will revert with a custom error if the contract is not found, so calling exists() is preferrable. Use tryGetContract to avoid error handling. The name provided is hashed to check in the registry.

params.name: The name of the contract to retrieve.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setContract(string,address).custom:events: ContractAdded(string name, address newAddress), ContractUpdated(string name, address oldAddress, address newAddress)
setContract(string,address).details: Only owner can call this function, and the address cann not be zero. The name set is hashed to create a unique key.

params.contractAddress: The address of the contract to register or update.
params.name: The name of the contract to register or update.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

tryGetContract(string).details: This function will return a boolean indicating if the contract was found, and the address of the contract.

params.name: The name of the contract to retrieve.

returns.contractAddress: The address of the contract, or address(0) if not found.
returns.found: A boolean indicating if the contract was found.

exists(string).notice: Checks if a contract exists in the registry by its name.

getContract(string).notice: Retrieves the address of a contract by its name.

setContract(string,address).notice: Registers or updates a contract address in the registry, and emits events

tryGetContract(string).notice: Tries to retrieve the address of a contract by its name. Check returned boolean to see if the contract was found.
